data.title: Miles Deutscher: The correlation between cryptocurrencies and technology stocks has reached a historical peak, and the digital gold narrative has been challenged
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Cryptocurrency analyst Miles Deutscher said that the correlation between cryptocurrencies and technology stocks has reached a historical peak. Unless proven otherwise, cryptocurrencies are just leveraged bets on risky assets, not digital gold.

data.title: Cryptocurrency's Correlation with Tech Stocks Reaches Historic High
data.description: According to Odaily, cryptocurrency analyst Miles Deutscher has noted that the correlation between cryptocurrencies and technology stocks has reached an all-time high. Deutscher suggests that unless proven otherwise, cryptocurrencies should be viewed as leveraged bets on risk assets rather than digital gold.

data.title: Bosera & HashKey to Launch First Tokenized ETFs to Bridge Crypto & TradFi
data.description:
TL;DR Bosera Asset Management and HashKey Group will launch the world’s first tokenized money market ETFs in April, with prior approval from Hong Kong’s financial regulator. The ETFs, denominated in HKD and USD, are part of “Project Ensemble,” a government initiative to explore real-world assets on the blockchain. This fusion of traditional funds and Web3. . . 
source: https://crypto-economy.com/bosera-hashkey-to-launch-first-tokenized-etfs-to-bridge-crypto-tradfi/

data.title: U.S. Core PCE Price Index Rises in February
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index increased by 2.8% year-over-year in February. This figure slightly exceeded the anticipated 2.7% growth rate.

data.title: The annual rate of the US core PCE price index in February was 2.8%, which was expected to be 2.7%.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The annual rate of the US core PCE price index in February was 2.8%, which was expected to be 2.7%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Galaxy Digital reaches $200 million settlement with New York prosecutors over LUNA investment case
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Cryptocurrency financial services company Galaxy Digital announced a $200 million settlement with the New York Attorney General's Office to settle its investigation into the collapse of the Terra-Luna ecosystem in 2022. Under the terms of the settlement, Galaxy will be responsible for its investments, transactions and public statements in LUNA tokens.
At the same time, Galaxy disclosed its 2024 financial report showing that the company achieved a net profit of $174 million in the fourth quarter and a full-year profit of $365 million (including settlement reserves). The company also announced a 15-year power supply agreement with cloud computing company CoreWeave, which will use its Helios data center in Texas to provide 133 megawatts of power to support AI and high-performance computing businesses, and is expected to generate $4.5 billion in revenue during the agreement period. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Mt. Gox protest sign to be auctioned, starting price 4.5 BTC
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The famous "Mt. Gox, Where Is Our Money?" handwritten protest slogan in the 2014 "Mentougou Incident" will be auctioned on the Scare.City platform on March 28, with a starting price of 4.5 BTC (about 383,000 US dollars). Holder Kolin Burges recalled that when he flew to Tokyo to protest, Mentougou representatives privately warned him that his behavior "may cause the exchange to collapse", but then he was refused payment when he used a Mentougou credit card to pay, indicating that the crisis was irreversible.
This auction coincided with the start of the repayment procedure for Mentougou's creditors. Former CEO Mark Karpeles founded a new exchange EllipX last year and claimed that "with modern blockchain tools, the tragedy could have been avoided." (CoinDesk)
